assessment.signage: Tin Sing International Trading Inc. bilingual signage; striped green awning

photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade is occupied and readable from the street, with contemporary storefront alterations layered onto the earlier building form.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple fascia and coping with limited decorative trim visible.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible from the selected frontage view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Character comes primarily from surviving massing, cladding, and current storefront treatment.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Closed or minimally active frontage with barred opening and plain lower facade treatment.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Large commercial sign board covers most of ground floor facade. Upper floor retains original red brick with residential windows. Parapet roofline with simple cornice.
photo_observations.notes: HCD typology is House-form, Semi-detached. The original house-form with residential upper floor is clearly legible above the commercial sign board. Red-brown brick and window proportions confirm Victorian-era construction.
photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: red-brown brick upper facade with large white commercial sign board (Tin Sing International Trading Inc.) at ground level
photo_observations.window_details: Two upper-floor windows with horizontal blinds/shutters visible. Ground floor has large commercial sign board covering most of the facade.
photo_observations.door_details: Two narrow street-level doors flank the barred display opening.
photo_observations.signage_observed: Tin Sing International Trading Inc. sign extends across the frontage zone.

facade_description: 155 Augusta Ave presents a house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
